headline: CONFIRMATION (anti-double-counting). April 23 voluntary buyout program (~7% US workforce / 8,750 employees, Rule of 70 eligibility, AI/Copilot teams exempt, details to employees May 7) was already absorbed in April 28 confirmation cycle at 66.4. No new acute April 24-29 net evidence beyond Fortune April 26 contextual analysis ('buyout dressed as a benefit'). Per anti-double-counting rule, re-assessing the same April 23 announcement does not generate a new proposal. Composite 66.4 (Established) holds.

evidence.finding: First-ever voluntary buyout in 51-year history; ~7% US workforce / 8,750 employees; Rule of 70 (age + tenure >= 70) eligibility; AI/Copilot teams exempt; details to employees May 7.
Combined Meta+Microsoft 20K cuts framed as AI-driven labor crisis. Microsoft mechanism is voluntary; Meta is direct layoff.
Contextual analysis: buyout positioned as workforce transition driven by AI automation rationale.

negativeSignals: Voluntary buyout still imposes structural workforce displacement at meaningful scale (8,750 employees); AI automation rationale persists.
positiveSignals: Voluntary mechanism with Rule of 70 eligibility is materially less coercive than direct layoff. AI/Copilot exemption signals strategic priority but not workforce-protection commitment. No retaliation pattern.
anti_double_counting_note: April 23 buyout was the basis for April 28 confirmation at 66.4. Per anti-double-counting rule, no new proposal generated unless materially new evidence emerges beyond the April 23 announcement.
